title: Berea Food Bank (Berea Faith Community Outreach)
description: An emergency food assistance program providing a week's worth of groceries to residents of Berea and southern Madison County. It directly provides nourishment to individuals and families facing food insecurity.

title: Berea Baptist Church Food Closet
description: A faith-based food pantry that provides supplemental food baskets to families in Berea. It serves as a vital nourishment resource for local residents.

title: Hope Food Pantry
description: Located in nearby Richmond, this pantry provides Madison County residents with direct access to an assortment of food and toiletries, fulfilling essential nourishment needs.

title: Salvation Army of Richmond
description: Provides a food pantry, free community meals, and emergency disaster services. It relates to nourishment through its food programs and shelter through its emergency housing assistance.

title: God's Outreach Madison County Food Bank
description: Offers supplemental food assistance to residents of Madison County once a month, serving as a critical pillar for community nourishment.

title: Appalachian Community Meal Project
description: A grassroots initiative connecting neighbors through community meals and neighborhood food pantries to ensure everyone has access to nourishment.

title: CELTS Better Together Food Drive
description: A community event hosted by Berea College's Center for Excellence in Learning through Service to raise funds and collect food for the Berea Food Bank, fighting food insecurity and providing nourishment.

title: Sustainable Berea & Berea Urban Farm
description: A community urban farm that grows fresh food (nourishment) and hosts the Agri-Culture Garden, which preserves the cultural stories and heritage of local food plants (a living art form).

title: Walmart Supercenter Berea
description: A major local grocery store providing fresh produce, dry goods, and everyday nourishment staples to the Berea community.

title: Save A Lot Berea
description: A discount grocery store in Berea offering affordable fresh food, meat, and pantry items to help families maintain nourishment on a budget.

title: Food Lion Berea
description: A local supermarket offering a wide variety of fresh food, baked goods, and household groceries to ensure daily nourishment.

title: Meijer Richmond
description: A large regional supermarket located in nearby Richmond that supplies fresh produce, meats, and comprehensive grocery options for community nourishment.

title: Kroger Richmond
description: A primary supermarket serving the Madison County area with fresh groceries, a pharmacy, and essential nourishment supplies.

title: MásGusto Mexican Grocery
description: A grocery delivery service offering authentic Mexican and Latin American ingredients, providing both nourishment and a connection to the culinary arts of Latin cultures.

title: Weee! Asian Grocery Delivery
description: Delivers authentic Japanese, Chinese, Korean, and Filipino groceries to Berea, offering diverse nourishment and celebrating the culinary arts of Asian cultures.

title: Berea Farmers Market
description: A year-round local market where regional farmers sell fresh produce, meats, and baked goods, directly connecting the community with healthy nourishment.

title: Room in the Inn Berea
description: A seasonal emergency shelter program that provides a safe, warm place to sleep for individuals experiencing homelessness in Berea during the winter months.

title: UP Initiative of Madison County
description: A drop-in warming and cooling center in Berea that offers shelter from the elements, alongside a clothing closet, laundry service, and food pantry (nourishment).

title: Madison Home
description: A seasonal emergency shelter in Richmond that provides overnight housing, job training, and addiction treatment resources for those without shelter.

title: Madison County Haven of Hope
description: A year-round emergency shelter serving Madison County, providing immediate housing support and resources to vulnerable populations.

title: New Liberty Family Shelter
description: A designated shelter facility in Richmond specifically designed to house and support families with children who are experiencing homelessness.

title: Beacon of Hope Emergency Shelter
description: Located in nearby Winchester, this shelter provides a safe and supportive housing environment for homeless individuals and families to rebuild their lives.

title: HOPE Center
description: A comprehensive facility offering emergency shelter, recovery programs, and daily meals (nourishment) to those facing homelessness in the broader Central Kentucky area.

title: Catholic Action Center
description: A 24-hour shelter providing lodging, food (nourishment), clothing, and laundry services to those in need, operating with no government funding.

title: Hope's Wings
description: A domestic violence program in Madison County that provides emergency housing and safe shelter for individuals fleeing abuse.

title: GreenHouse17
description: An advocacy agency and emergency shelter supporting survivors of domestic violence in the region, offering safe housing and crisis intervention.

title: Kentucky River Foothills Development Council
description: Provides housing assistance, including the Eastern Scholar House program in Richmond which offers affordable housing and childcare for single parents pursuing education.

title: Berea Home Village
description: A nonprofit network that provides volunteers and services (like home repairs and transportation) to help the elderly safely maintain shelter and age in their own homes.

title: Berea Health Care Center
description: A senior living and rehabilitation complex offering elderly individuals specialized housing, medical care, and a safe shelter environment.

title: The Terrace Nursing & Rehabilitation Center
description: A senior living facility in Berea providing dedicated housing, physical therapy, and long-term shelter for the elderly.

title: Kenwood Health And Rehabilitation Center
description: An elderly home in Madison County that provides senior living accommodations, grounds upkeep, and medical shelter for up to 93 residents.

title: McCready Manor
description: An independent living and senior housing apartment complex in Richmond, offering a safe, community-focused shelter for older adults.

title: Arcadian Cove
description: A senior living and assisted living facility in Richmond providing secure housing, amenities, and daily care support.

title: Berea Police Department Transient Emergency Assistance
description: Partnered with local outreach, the police department provides short-term emergency help and shelter connections for travelers facing breakdowns or crises.

title: Berea City Park
description: A public park offering physical picnic shelters for community gatherings, as well as beautifully maintained recreational space that hosts local arts and craft fairs.

title: Memorial Park Berea
description: Berea's oldest park, featuring a large and small picnic shelter for public use, acting as a passive greenspace and community refuge.

title: Fort Boonesborough State Park
description: Offers camping shelters and historic fort housing replicas (shelter), while serving as a hub for historic pioneer craft demonstrations and the Kentucky River Museum (arts).

title: Union City Neighborhood Park
description: A local community park in Richmond providing a public pavilion/shelter, historical walking trails, and playgrounds for families.

title: Lake Reba Park
description: A massive 75-acre park complex featuring public picnic shelters, ADA-friendly kayak launches, and the Judy Rains Memorial Dog Park (a dedicated shelter/play space for pets).

title: Irvine-McDowell Park
description: Provides shady picnic shelters and recreational areas, while also housing the Irvinton House Museum right inside the park (fine arts/history).

title: Kentucky Artisan Center at Berea
description: A premier state facility featuring the work of over 800 Kentucky artisans, showcasing everything from traditional woodwork and pottery to modern fine arts.

title: Artisan Village (Old Town Berea)
description: The historic heart of Berea featuring cobblestone streets lined with working artist studios, galleries, and craft shops, cementing Berea's title as the Folk Arts and Crafts Capital.

title: Top Drawer Gallery
description: A curated art gallery in the Artisan Village offering fine handmade artistry in jewelry, glass, pottery, wood, and metal from over 80 regional artists.

title: Berea College Doris Ulmann Galleries
description: A fine arts gallery on the Berea College campus hosting historical art collections, rotating student exhibitions, and regional cultural artifacts.

title: Log House Craft Gallery
description: The oldest craft gallery in Kentucky, selling fine arts and crafts created directly by the students of Berea College's historic Student Craft program.

title: Russel Acton Folk Center
description: A community facility dedicated to preserving and hosting folk arts, traditional dances, musical performances, and cultural gatherings.

title: EKU Center for the Arts
description: Central Kentucky's premier performing arts venue located in Richmond, hosting national Broadway tours, international ballets, and world-class musical concerts.

title: Richmond Area Arts Center
description: A cultural hub offering youth arts programs, adult creative workshops, and local art exhibits for the Madison County community.

title: Spotlight Playhouse
description: A highly active local community theater in Berea that produces plays, musicals, and performing arts education for local talent and audiences.

title: First Friday Berea
description: A monthly community block party that heavily features local musicians, craft vendors (fine arts), and an array of local food trucks and farmers (nourishment).

title: Warren A. May Woodworker
description: A renowned local studio in Berea where a master craftsman creates traditional Appalachian dulcimers and fine wood furniture.

title: Gastineau Studio
description: A staple of the Berea arts community offering high-quality, handcrafted jewelry and visual arts pieces made by resident artists.

title: Family Tree Gallery
description: Located on Broadway in Berea, this shop curates traditional Appalachian crafts, fine arts, and heritage goods from local creators.

title: Appalachian Shakespeare Company
description: A theatrical organization based at Eastern Kentucky University blending professional and amateur actors to bring the performing arts to the region.

title: Madison Community Band
description: An intergenerational musical ensemble that performs free concerts at local parks and community centers, fostering musical arts in Madison County.
